## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On October 17, 1973, an incredibly massive, historically definitive, and absolutely catastrophic global economic weapon was aggressively deployed. The Organization of Arab Petroleum Exporting Countries (OAPEC) officially and aggressively initiated the massive 1973 Oil Embargo. Heavily targeting the United States and its massive allies for their aggressive support of Israel during the Yom Kippur War, this incredibly massive supply shock quadrupled global oil prices, fundamentally shattering massive Western economies and heavily redefining global geopolitics.

On October 17, 1979, Mother Teresa, the incredibly massive, universally revered Catholic nun, was officially and aggressively awarded the incredibly massive Nobel Peace Prize. This incredibly massive global recognition heavily honored her absolute, decades-long, and fiercely selfless dedication to aggressively serving the absolute poorest of the poor in the massive slums of Calcutta, India. Her incredibly massive, profound humanitarian work fundamentally propelled her to absolute, global saintly immortality.
